Symmetry74↑ 8

Movement Signature — Glider

Smooth, low-oscillation stride with efficient ground contact. Gliders conserve energy well but can under-recruit hip drive at higher speeds.

Your Edge

Elite-level vertical oscillation control — top 15% of Couro runners for stride smoothness.

Areas to Develop

Hip extension at toe-off and left-side knee drive lag the rest of the signature.

The Reveal · one stride, seen completely

Best track session on record — symmetry finally clicked

From a few seconds of phone video, Couro tracked thousands of joint positions — 33 landmarks on every frame, every one of them hers. This is the second nobody can see at full speed. Jul 6 · Track Sprint · Glider

0Couro Score
↑ 84cadence symmetry
↓ 61knee drive
238contact time ms

SINCE HER LAST SESSION · JUN 22

Cadence symmetry79 → 84 ▲
Knee drive58 → 61 ▲
Contact time242 → 238 ms ▲
Crossover (L)3.6 → 3.4 cm ●

Three moved. The fix target barely did — that's why crossover is this week's mission.

📌 CALLED IT · FROM HER JUN 18 REPORT

“Symmetry should click within three sessions if the hitch work holds.”Couro's call, two reports ago
84landed · personal best

Every report ends with a call on the record. The last one landed — this report's new call is at the bottom of the page.

THE BREAKTHROUGH

Symmetry finally clicked

843 sessions climbing

Cadence symmetry hit 84 — her best ever. The right-side hitch from spring sessions is gone, and it held for the full session.

cadence symmetry · green = target band

but one pattern is pushing back…

THE BOTTLENECK

Left foot still crosses midline

572 sessions flat

On the back straight her left foot lands 3–4 cm across midline, loading the adductor chain and capping knee drive at 61.

foot crossover (L) · dot sits below band

and here's what fixing it unlocks…

THE UNLOCK

~4 points of score head-room

86projected

Runners with her profile who clean up crossover typically gain 3–5 symmetry points within six weeks — enough to move her into the elite band.

ONE FIX BREAKS THE CHAIN

Knee Drive 61 → 70

Free the left knee and the whole chain reorganizes.

Wall knee-drive holds
3 × 20 s each side
A-skip ladder
4 × 30 m
Lane-line strides
4 × 60 m

How Couro Measures

A note on how we frame risk

Couro's metrics are part of a broader load-management picture, not deterministic injury predictors. Injury risk is multifactorial — training load, history, sleep, nutrition, and biomechanics all play a role. We flag mechanical attention levels, not diagnoses.

Based on: Buist et al. (2008), Umbrella Systematic Review (2024), IOC FAIR Consensus (2025)

Add a squad

Name the team and paste your athletes. Profiles belong to your organization, not to the team — so an athlete on two teams stays one profile with one history, and anyone on your staff can film them.

1 · Team details
2 · Add your athletes · one profile per person, org-wide

Two ways: upload your roster spreadsheet (Excel — fastest, and if it has height & weight the profiles come out already personalized), or paste names below. If the sheet has a Student ID, re-importing next term updates those athletes rather than duplicating them — and a Team column creates teams as it goes.

Upload your roster (Excel)

.xlsx or .csv · Couro maps the columns for you. PDF works too — best-effort.

0 athletes · 0 new profiles
Names appear here as you type — check the list, remove any typo.
3 · Personalize · optional — add anytime

📏 Height & weight personalize the targets. Couro scales what "good" looks like to each athlete's frame — stride, ground-contact, cadence and joint load all shift with size. A name alone still works — Couro scores it against sport defaults and flags the report estimated until you add the numbers. Fill them now, or let athletes complete their own when they claim their profile.

Add athletes above, then fill in whatever you have — every field is optional.

Roster—
Sport & groupTrack — Sprints

🔗
How athletes get their own profile

You create the profiles; each athlete claims theirs from a link you share (text, QR, or your team app). Claiming links their account, so they see their own reports and can fill in anything you left blank. Coach-created, athlete-owned.

New Run Analysis

for Montana Carlson

For
Three taps, about 30 seconds. Pick the move · drop a clip · analyze.
1 · What are they doing?
2 · Add the video

📱 One phone, filmed side-on, full body in frame. 4–10 seconds is plenty — Couro auto-detects the speed and the rest. Front/back angles are optional bonuses.

or upload an existing clip
Compare fresh vs fatigued? Add a second clip and Couro lines them up to show what fades — optional, skip it to just get today's read.
Advanced · pick the assessment gear (auto-detected by default)

Couro auto-detects the speed bucket from average velocity. Only override it if you're deliberately testing a specific gear.

AthleteMontana Carlson
SessionTrack
Videosno video yet
Comparison—
ResultReport ready in ~1 min · uses 1 credit
TrackAuto-detect
The whole team in about two minutes. Film once · confirm the cuts · analyze.
How Couro knows who's who
You never cut a single clip. You tell Couro the order once, then confirm the faces.
1
Film in one passLine the team up and film each rep back-to-back — one clip, up to a minute.
2
Set the orderDrag your roster into the order they went. That's the key — clip 3 becomes your 3rd athlete.how it names them
3
Couro cuts & namesIt finds every rep and tags each with the right name automatically.
4
Confirm the facesGlance at each freeze-frame. Wrong person? One tap swaps the name.you verify
1 · Team & video
TeamWestgate — Sprints12 athletes
or upload a clip you already filmed
Drop one continuous video

Line them up and film back-to-back — Couro finds each rep and cuts it for you. Keep it tight: up to 1 minute · MP4 / MOV

Required
2 · Tell Couro who's who · the order they went in

Drag each athlete into the order they performed. Couro names every clip from this list — the 1st rep it finds becomes your 1st athlete, and so on. No jerseys or tagging needed; you'll double-check faces next.

3 · Confirm who's who · glance at each face, fix any mismatch

Each clip shows a freeze-frame with the name Couro assigned. Wrong person? Tap the name to swap it. Drop any clip that isn't a real rep, and mark anyone Couro missed.

Who gets assessed Couro found 18 people in this video — your 12 athletes plus bystanders, an official and the opposing bench.
12 assessed6 skipped
Skipped people are dropped before analysis, not after — so you aren't billed for assessments you didn't want, and the video comes back sooner.

Analyzing 12 reps in parallel…

Each confirmed clip runs as its own job — so the whole team finishes in about the time of one report. Reports land on the team board as they complete.

Auto-cut & matched✓

$30/month

$49/month

Athletes belong to the organization, not to a coach. Every staff member here sees the same roster — nobody re-enters anyone, and a two-sport athlete stays one profile with one history.
Staffwho can see and edit this roster
CV Cam Van (you)cam@couro.io Owner
DR Dana Ruizdruiz@westgate.edu
SI Sam Iheanachosami@westgate.edu
AM Alex Moreauamoreau@westgate.edu

Staff upload, assess and edit the roster. Admins can also add people and change roles. An organization always keeps at least one owner or admin, so it can never be left with nobody who can administer it.

Add colleaguesone link, shared however your staff already talk
Fall staff joins as staff used 0× expires 3 Aug 2026
https://team.couro.io/join/8f3c2a91-4b7e-4d02-9c5a-1e6b0d7f2a44

A link needs no email address, nothing to deliver, and nothing to match against whatever address they eventually sign up with. The safeguards sit on the link: it expires in 14 days, it can be revoked, and its role is fixed when created — so a link that leaks grants staff, never ownership.

Not the same as an athlete claim link. This adds a coach to your staff. A claim link lets an athlete claim their own profile and see their reports.

Couro

Join an organization

This link shares an athlete roster with you. Joining adds this account — druiz@westgate.edu — to Westgate Athletics, as staff.

  • You see the same 912 athletes everyone else does
  • Nothing to re-enter — no roster to rebuild
  • Your uploads stay yours if you ever leave

Not now Joining is a button, not the link itself — so a preview in Slack can't join you by accident.

Westgate Softball · Pitching · Coach Dana Ruiz · In-season

Westgate Softball — Pitchers

Team movement strategyWestgate Softball — Pitchers · 14 athletes
The read

Your pitchers win on trunk stability — but 3 share a late-trunk-rotation pattern quietly loading the throwing shoulder.

🎯 One moveSequence-fix those 3 before season load peaks — Rosa's arm-path drift is the early warning. The same fix protects all three shoulders.

Where the pitchers carry load · across all 14

Throwing shoulder3 elevated — late trunk rotation
Follow-throughdecel −9 — team-wide watch
Trunk & core11 strong — your identity
💡The shoulder load isn't random — it traces to one sequencing fault in 3 named pitchers. Fixable upstream, before it reaches the joint.

How the pitchers move · movement strategies

Hips-lead sequenced9
MCABJF+6
Your identity — trunk leads, arm follows clean.
Early-trunk (arm lags)3
RDETBE
Shared risk — trunk opens early, shoulder pays.
Developing2
PRKC
Freshmen — signature still forming.

Arm path is clean earlyThrough the first 40 pitches, mechanics are textbook across the group — the problem is late, not baseline.
✓
Trending up 4 weeks straight+3 on the team score this cycle. Whatever changed in March is compounding.

Top 3 to address

1
Late-outing fatigue → arm-path drift5 of 14 · same signature in five pitchers — arm path drifts and trunk collapses late as workload climbs. One shared fix: decel circuit + workload spacing.
2
Follow-through cut short4 of 14 · decel under 65. Long-toss holds + cue "finish past the knee."
3
Trunk tilt under fatigue3 of 14 · shows up late-inning only. Anti-rotation core block + re-test.

Measurement scope
Scored — validated for rear viewtrunk tilt · arm path · follow-through deceleration · L/R symmetry
Not scored herestride length · leg block · landing — these need a side angle. We don't estimate what one camera can't see.
The Reveal System CONCEPT

Every session opens with its own reveal

One formula, seven signatures: the image only that camera angle can produce · a vast, true number · one intimate line · the score, counted up.

TRACK · SIDELIVE

One stride, seen completely

Nine positions from a single stride — thousands of joint positions from one short phone clip. This is the reveal shipping in the report today.

TRACK · FRONTCONCEPT

The mirror test

Left vs right: two runners in one body. Every landing is judged against a perfect mirror — the crossover cannot hide from this angle.

TRACK · BACKCONCEPT

Two rails

Every footstrike of the session, mapped from behind. Strides should ride two rails — the dots that wander off them are the story.

TREADMILLCONCEPT

Every stride, stacked into one

Continuous capture is volume: the whole session overlaid. Consistency reads as one sharp line — fatigue reads as blur.

BASKETBALLCONCEPT

Frozen at the apex

Flight is measured in tenths of a second — and decided in the ground contact before takeoff. Freeze the apex, then read the story backwards.

FREEFLOWCONCEPT

Your signature

Any movement, anywhere. Every joint draws a light-path through space — no two athletes sign their motion the same way.

SOFTBALL · REARCONCEPT

The circle

The arm circle traced end to end from the angle coaches actually film — path, timing, and the release, in one loop.

💡Side track ships today. Each other mode needs exactly one renderer output — then the same hero component, the same formula, the same goosebumps.

Sample athlete evidence frames (real Couro capture) — demo session values shown.
